methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Hyfix is a seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $15M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public companies operating in the same sector and at similar or adjacent stages, including private peers Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $15M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage step-ups, implying a $38M valuation. The peer-median method benchmarks against four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Tenna Systems ($60M seed), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), and Stark Defence ($500M Series A). The median of this cohort is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

The two methods yield divergent results, reflecting inherent uncertainty in early-stage valuation. The triangulated range is $32M (low), $131M (median), to $259M (high). The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Hyfix's technology maturity, customer traction, and competitive positioning relative to later-stage peers in the comp set. Stage-progression suggests conservative near-term valuation; peer-median suggests the company could command higher multiples if it demonstrates defensible advantages. Additional clarity on product-market fit and customer pipeline would narrow this range materially.
